unverified: string[];\n   713\t  };\n   714\t  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n   715\t  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];\n   716\t  recommendedFixScope:\n   717\t    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n   718\t    | \"quick-fix\"\n   719\t    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n   720\t    | \"human-ruling\";\n   721\t  openQuestions: string[];\n   722\t  additionalObservations: string[];\n   723\t}\n   724\t```\n   725\t\n   726\t```typescript\n   727\texport type FindingSeverity = \"critical\" | \"major\" | \"minor\" | \"observation\";\n   728\texport type RequirementId = string;\n   729\t\n   730\texport interface VerifierFinding {\n   731\t  id: string;\n   732\t  severity: FindingSeverity;\n   733\t  title: string;\n   734\t  evidence: string;\n   735\t  affectedFiles: string[];\n   736\t  requirementIds: RequirementId[];\n   737\t  recommendedFixScope:\n   738\t    | \"same-session-implementor\"\n   739\t    | \"quick-fix\"\n   740\t    | \"fresh-fix-path\"\n   741\t    | \"human-ruling\";\n   742\t  blocking: boolean;\n   743\t}\n   744\t```\n   745\t\n   746\t### Quick Fix Result Contract\n   747\t\n   748\t```typescript\n   749\texport interface QuickFixResult {\n   750\t  resultId: string;\n   751\t  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n   752\t  model: string;\n   753\t  outcome:\n   754\t    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n   755\t    | \"needs-more-routing\"\n   756\t    | \"blocked\";\n   757\t  filesChanged: string[];\n   758\t  changeSummary: string;\n   759\t  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n   760\t  unresolvedConcerns: string[];\n   761\t  recommendedNextStep: string;\n   762\t}\n   763\t```\n   764\t\n   765\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   766\t\n   767\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   768\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   769\t| TC-3.3a | verifier insert applied | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | verifier insert fixture | verifier prompt includes insert |\n   770\t| TC-3.3b | verifier insert omitted cleanly | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | absent insert | prompt still valid |\n   771\t| TC-3.4b | verifier reading journey included | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | verifier input | evidence-focused reading instructions included |\n   772\t| TC-3.4c | quick-fix handoff stays narrow | verifier launches requested |\n   774\t| TC-5.1b | re-verification uses fresh sessions | `provider-adapter.test.ts` | prior verifier session ids | new verifier sessions used |\n   775\t| TC-5.2a | verifier contract complete | `result-contracts.test.ts` | sample verifier result | schema requires identity, findings, coverage, gates |\n   776\t| TC-5.2b | additional observations retained | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | provider returns soft observations | result includes additional observations |\n   777\t| TC-5.2c | mock/shim audit required | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | integration-facing fixture | mock audit field included and populated when needed |\n   778\t| TC-5.3a | quick-fix routing supported | `quick-fix-command.test.ts` | bounded fix request | quick-fix operation executes without full implementor path |\n   779\t| TC-5.3b | quick-fix contract explicit | `result-contracts.test.ts` | quick-fix result fixture | schema requires summary, files, unresolved concerns |\n   780\t| TC-5.4a | implementor uncertainty preserved for orchestrator | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | uncertain implementor output | surfaced in result |\n   781\t| TC-5.4b | verifier disagreement preserved | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | conflicting verifier results | batch result indicates revise/block instead of silent resolution |\n   782\t| TC-5.5a | final story gate not owned by CLI | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | public docs state gate is orchestrator-owned |\n   783\t\n   784\t## Flow 4: Epic Cleanup, Verification, and Synthesis\n   785\t\n   786\t**Covers:** AC-7.1 to AC-7.3, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4\n   787\t\n   788\tAt epic closeout the CLI executes three different kinds of work: cleanup-only correction, epic-level verifier batches, and synthesis. The orchestrator still determines whether the run can close. The CLI should never blur these responsibilities into one vague “finish the epic” command.\n   789\t\n   790\tThe cleanup operation is distinct because it acts on an orchestrator-curated cleanup batch. It is not a general-purpose retry. It is a bounded correction-only workflow designed to start epic verification from the cleaned state rather than from an accumulation of known-but-deferred issues.\n   791\t\n   792\t```mermaid\n   793\tsequenceDiagram\n   794\t    participant Orchestrator\n   795\t    participant CLI\n   796\t    participant Cleanup as Cleanup Runner\n   797\t    participant EV as Epic Verifier Batch\n   798\t    participant SYN as Epic Synthesizer\n   799\t\n   800\t    Note over Orchestrator,SYN: AC-7.1 to AC-8.4\n   801\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-cleanup --batch <cleanup.md>\n   802\t    CLI->>Cleanup: apply approved cleanup items\n   803\t    Cleanup-->>CLI: cleanup result\n   804\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: cleanup outcome\n   805\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-verify\n   806\t    CLI->>EV: launch epic verifiers\n   807\t    EV-->>CLI: epic verifier results\n   808\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch\n   809\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-synthesize\n   810\t    CLI->>SYN: verify and consolidate findings\n   811\t    SYN-->>CLI: synthesis result\n   812\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: synthesis outcome\n   813\t```\n   814\t\n   815\t### Epic Verifier Result\n   816\t\n   817\t```typescript\n   818\texport interface EpicVerifierResult {\n   819\t  resultId: string;\n   820\t  outcome: \"pass\" | \"revise\" | \"block\";\n   821\t  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n   822\t  model: string;\n   823\t  reviewerLabel: string;\n   824\t  crossStoryFindings: string[];\n   825\t  architectureFindings: string[];\n   826\t  epicCoverageAssessment: string[];\n   827\t  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];\n   828\t  blockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];\n   829\t  nonBlockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];\n   830\t  unresolvedItems: string[];\n   831\t  gateResult: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\";\n   832\t}\n   833\t```\n   834\t\n   835\t### Epic Cleanup Result\n   836\t\n   837\t```typescript\n   838\texport interface EpicCleanupResult {\n   839\t  resultId: string;\n   840\t  outcome: \"cleaned\" | \"needs-more-cleanup\" | \"blocked\";\n   841\t  cleanupBatchPath: string;\n   842\t  filesChanged: string[];\n   843\t  changeSummary: string;\n   844\t  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n   845\t  unresolvedConcerns: string[];\n   846\t  recommendedNextStep: string;\n   847\t}\n   848\t```\n   849\t\n   850\t### Epic Synthesis Result\n   851\t\n   852\t```typescript\n   853\texport interface EpicSynthesisResult {\n   854\t  resultId: string;\n   855\t  outcome:\n   856\t    | \"ready-for-closeout\"\n   857\t    | \"needs-fixes\"\n   858\t    | \"needs-more-verification\"\n   859\t    | \"blocked\";\n   860\t  confirmedIssues: string[];\n   861\t  disputedOrUnconfirmedIssues: string[];\n   862\t  readinessAssessment: string;\n   863\t  recommendedNextStep: string;\n   864\t}\n   865\t```\n   866\t\n   867\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   868\t\n   869\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   870\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   871\t| TC-7.1a | cleanup result contract exists | `epic-cleanup-command.test.ts` | cleanup batch fixture | cleanup command returns durable result |\n   872\t| TC-7.2a | cleanup stage preserves review-before-dispatch boundary | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | docs require human review prior to cleanup execution |\n   873\t| TC-7.3a | cleanup verified before epic verification | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | process documentation requires cleanup completion before epic verification begins |\n   874\t| TC-8.1a | epic verification mandatory | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | closeout docs fixture | no closeout path without `epic-verify` |\n   875\t| TC-8.1b | no skip path | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | public command flow exposes no skip route |\n   876\t| TC-8.1c | epic mock/shim audit present | `epic-verify-command.test.ts` | production-path fixture | epic verifier result includes mock audit field |\n   877\t| TC-8.2a | synthesis mandatory | `epic-synthesize-command.test.ts` | verifier results fixture | synthesis command required and returns structured result |\n   878\t| TC-8.3a | synthesis verifies rather than merges blindly | `epic-synthesize-command.test.ts` | conflicting verifier reports | synthesis distinguishes confirmed vs unconfirmed issues |\n   879\t| TC-8.4a | final epic gate remains orchestrator-owned | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | docs state final gate is outside CLI |\n   880\t\n   881\t## Provider Adapters\n   882\t\n   883\tThe provider adapters let the CLI execute the same operation contract against different harnesses without changing the orchestrator-facing command surface.\n   884\t\n   885\t```typescript\n   886\texport interface ProviderExecutionRequest {\n   887\t  prompt: string;\n   888\t  cwd: string;\n   889\t  model: string;\n   890\t  reasoningEffort: string;\n   891\t  resumeSessionId?: string;\n   892\t  timeoutMs: number;\n   893\t}\n   894\t\n   895\texport interface ProviderExecutionResult {\n   896\t  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n   897\t  sessionId?: string;\n   898\t  stdout: string;\n   899\t  stderr: string;\n   900\t  exitCode: number;\n   901\t  parsedResult?: unknown;\n   902\t}\n   903\t\n   904\texport interface ProviderAdapter {\n   905\t  checkAvailability(): Promise<HarnessAvailability>;\n   906\t  execute(request: ProviderExecutionRequest): Promise<ProviderExecutionResult>;\n   907\t}\n   908\t```\n   909\t\n   910\tAdapter rules:\n   911\t\n   912\t- `claude-code` adapter uses the primary harness path and supports “no secondary harness” roles.\n   913\t- `codex` adapter supports runtime model and reasoning-effort overrides.\n   914\t- `copilot` adapter supports runtime model override, but reasoning effort is effectively config-level and should be treated as advisory in validation notes.\n   915\t- Adapters must return explicit session IDs when the provider exposes them. The CLI should never rely on “latest session for cwd” behavior.\n   916\t\n   917\t### Provider Invocation Contracts\n   918\t\n   919\tThe adapter behavior must be explicit per harness.\n   920\t\n   921\t| Harness | Invocation Shape | Resume Shape | Notes |\n   922\t|---|---|---|---|\n   923\t| `codex` | `codex exec --json -m <model> -c model_reasoning_effort=<effort> \"<prompt>\"` | `codex exec resume --json <session-id> \"<follow-up prompt>\"` | use explicit session IDs only; capture stdout to artifact before parsing |\n   924\t| `copilot` | `copilot -p \"<prompt>\" -s --model <model> ...` with adapter-owned result capture and permission flags | no session resume contract for retained implementor in v1; use fresh programmatic runs only | Copilot is valid for fresh-session roles only in v1; reasoning effort treated as advisory because it is config-level |\n   925\t| `claude-code` | `claude -p \"<prompt>\" --output-format json --model <model> --permission-mode <mode>` | `claude -p \"<follow-up prompt>\" --resume <session-id> --output-format json --model <model>` | do not reuse the live interactive orchestrator session; do not rely on `--continue` or latest-session-by-cwd behavior |\n   926\t\n   927\tThe v1 design assumes the primary harness is still invokable as a child process. If that proves false in implementation, the design must be updated explicitly rather than allowing the runtime model to drift into “emit a prompt and ask the orchestrator to run it manually.”\n   928\t\n   929\t### `story-continue` Resolution Rules\n   930\t\n   931\t`story-continue` resolves `model` and `reasoningEffort` from the active `story_implementor` entry in the selected config file unless a future continuation-handle schema explicitly carries immutable model metadata. The continuation handle is intentionally light in v1 and should not become a second config surface.\n   932\t\n   933\t### Availability and Auth Checks\n   934\t\n   935\t| Harness | Version Check | Auth Check | Availability Tier Notes |\n   936\t|---|---|---|---|\n   937\t| `claude-code` | `claude --version` | `claude auth status` | supports `binary-present` and `authenticated-known` tiers |\n   938\t| `codex` | `codex --version` | explicit auth-state command if available, otherwise mark auth as `unknown` without paid smoke by default | supports `binary-present`; `authenticated-known` only if a non-mutating auth check exists |\n   939\t| `copilot` | `copilot --version` | explicit auth-state command if available, otherwise mark auth as `unknown` without paid smoke by default | `--npx` fallback deferred in v1 |\n   940\t\n   941\tAvailability tiers:\n   942\t\n   943\t- `binary-present`\n   944\t- `authenticated-known`\n   945\t- `auth-unknown`\n   946\t- `unavailable`\n   947\t\n   948\t### Provider Output Parsing Rules\n   949\t\n   950\t- Provider stdout is captured in full to a temp/result artifact before parsing.\n   951\t- The adapter validates the parsed provider result before converting it into the command result envelope.\n   952\t- Invalid structured output becomes a machine-readable CLI error with a stable error code.\n   953\t- Missing session IDs are treated as blocking for commands that require continuation support.\n   954\t- Provider stderr may be persisted for debugging, but it is not the orchestration source of truth.\n   955\t\n   956\tFor v1, provider prompts require the model to return exactly one JSON object matching the operation payload schema. The adapter accepts only:\n   957\t\n   958\t1. provider-native JSON whose final text/result field is exactly that object, or\n   959\t2. raw stdout that is exactly that object.\n   960\t\n   961\tThe adapter does not parse arbitrary prose, markdown summaries, or fenced JSON blocks in v1. Invalid output returns `PROVIDER_OUTPUT_INVALID`.\n   962\t\n   963\t## Verification Gate Resolution\n   964\t\n   965\tThe runtime must not guess gate commands ad hoc.\n   966\t\n   967\t1. explicit command flags\n   968\t2. explicit run-config values, if later added\n   969\t3. discovered package scripts\n   970\t4. supporting policy docs or CI config\n   971\t5. otherwise `needs-user-decision`\n   972\t\n   973\t```typescript\n   974\texport interface VerificationGateConfig {\n   975\t  storyGate: string;\n   976\t  epicGate: string;\n   977\t  source: \"cli-flag\" | \"run-config\" | \"package-scripts\" | \"policy-doc\" | \"ci-config\";\n   978\t}\n   979\t```\n   980\t\n   981\tThe preflight result should surface this config so the orchestrator can persist it into `team-impl-log.md`.\n   982\t\n   983\t### Canonical Gate Mapping\n   984\t\n   985\tUntil a project overrides the defaults, the expected mapping for this repo is:\n   986\t\n   987\t| Gate | Command |\n   988\t|---|---|\n   989\t| Story gate | `bun run green-verify` |\n   990\t| Epic gate | `bun run verify-all` |\n   991\t\n   992\tThis mapping should appear consistently in:\n   993\t\n   994\t- `preflight` output\n   995\t- `team-impl-log.md`\n   996\t- prompt assembly inputs\n   997\t- process-playbook examples\n   998\t\n   999\t## Packaging and Build Integration\n  1000\t\n  1001\tThe source runtime stays modular. The packaged skill should contain one bundled Node-compatible JS file.\n  1002\t\n  1003\t### Build Steps\n  1004\t\n  1005\t1. Author prompt assets under `processes/impl-cli/prompts/`.\n  1006\t2. Run `scripts/sync-impl-cli-assets.ts` to generate `embedded-assets.generated.ts`.\n  1007\t3. Bundle `processes/impl-cli/cli.ts` with Bun targeting Node-compatible output.\n  1008\t4. Copy the bundled artifact into `dist/skills/ls-claude-impl/bin/`.\n  1009\t5. Validate that the packaged skill includes `SKILL.md`, all reference files, and the runtime artifact.\n  1010\t\n  1011\t### Bundling Notes\n  1012\t\n  1013\t- Keep the build target Node-compatible.\n  1014\t- Prefer a single JS runtime artifact in the packaged skill.\n  1015\t- Embed prompt assets into the bundle to avoid distributing a second runtime prompt tree inside the skill.\n  1016\t- Leave provider CLIs external. docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-00/006-verifier-codex53-high.jsonl:65:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_32","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba /Users/leemoore/code/liminal-spec/src/references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md'","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Claude Impl CLI Operations\n     2\t\n     3\tThis guide explains the public CLI from the orchestrator's perspective. The CLI handles one bounded operation at a time and returns a structured envelope that the orchestrator records in `team-impl-log.md`.\n     4\t\n     5\t## Command Map\n     6\t\n     7\t| Command | Use It When | Outcome States | What To Do Next |\n     8\t|---|---|---|---|\n     9\t| `inspect` | You need to confirm the spec-pack layout, tech-design shape, story inventory, and insert presence. | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` | Record the layout and continue to the reading journey or pause for a user decision. |\n    10\t| `preflight` | The reading journey is complete and `impl-run.config.json` exists. | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` | Record the validated configuration and gate choices before story work. |\n    11\t| `story-implement` | A story is ready to start. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` | Route verification or follow-up work based on the returned outcome. |\n    12\t| `story-continue` | The active retained implementor session should continue. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` | Continue the same story session only when the continuation handle matches. |\n    13\t| `story-verify` | Fresh verifiers should review one story. | `pass`, `revise`, `block` | Route fixes or move toward acceptance. |\n    14\t| `quick-fix` | A small, bounded fix should run without restarting the full implementor workflow. | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-more-routing`, `blocked` | Re-verify or route a larger follow-up path. |\n    15\t| `epic-cleanup` | Approved cleanup-only fixes should run before epic verification. | `cleaned`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `blocked` | Review the cleanup result before epic verification continues. |\n    16\t| `epic-verify` | All stories are accepted and cleanup is complete. | `pass`, `revise`, `block` | Gather the verifier batch for synthesis. |\n    17\t| `epic-synthesize` | Epic verifier results are available and need consolidation. | `ready-for-closeout`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`, `blocked` | Run the final orchestrator-owned gate or route more work. |\n    18\t\n    19\t## IO Contract\n    20\t\n    21\t- With `--json`, stdout must contain exactly one JSON envelope.\n    22\t- The same envelope is persisted under `<spec-pack-root>/artifacts/`.\n    23\t- `stderr` is for progress or debugging only; it is not the routing source of truth.\n    24\t\n    25\t## Ownership Boundary\n    26\t\n    27\tThe final story gate and final epic gate remain orchestrator-owned. The CLI can report readiness, verification findings, and cleanup outcomes, but it does not accept stories or close the epic by itself.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The feature has two dense domains that need different levels of treatment: the orchestrator-facing skill surface and the CLI/runtime surface that executes bounded operations for multiple secondary harnesses. For that reason this design uses **Config B**:\n     6\t\n     7\t| Document | Purpose |\n     8\t|---|---|\n     9\t| `tech-design.md` | Decision record, context, system view, top-tier surfaces, module architecture overview, dependency and packaging decisions, work breakdown |\n    10\t| `tech-design-skill-process.md` | The orchestrator-facing skill surface: progressive disclosure docs, reading journey, state ownership, process playbook, stage transitions |\n    11\t| `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` | The CLI/runtime surface: command model, config schemas, prompt asset system, provider adapters, operation contracts, packaging |\n    12\t| `test-plan.md` | TC-to-test mapping, mock strategy, fixture strategy, test counts, chunk-level TDD plan |\n    13\t\n    14\tThe index stays small enough to be navigable while still recording the cross-cutting decisions that both companion docs inherit. The companion split follows the real boundary in this feature: one side teaches the orchestrator how the process works, the other side executes the process through a clean agent-first CLI.\n    15\t\n    16\t## Spec Validation\n    17\t\n    18\tThe epic is designable. Its major flows, ACs, and TCs describe a coherent system with clear public seams: spec-pack discovery, run configuration, prompt assembly, retained implementation sessions, fresh verification, cleanup, and epic closeout. The epic is unusually detailed for a skill feature, but the added specificity is appropriate because the feature is not just a prose skill; it is a skill plus a runtime helper plus a prompt system plus a result-contract surface.\n    19\t\n    20\tThe design does make three clarifications that should be recorded explicitly before implementation begins. None of them block design, but each one turns an implied behavior into a concrete contract the implementation can build around.\n    21\t\n    22\t| Issue | Spec Location | Resolution | Status |\n    23\t|---|---|---|---|\n    24\t| The epic requires the orchestrator to record role defaults and run choices, but it does not name a dedicated machine-readable run config file. | AC-2.4, Data Contracts, `team-impl-log.md` requirements | Introduce `impl-run.config.json` at the spec-pack root as the orchestrator-owned run configuration. `team-impl-log.md` remains the durable narrative/state record. | Resolved — clarified |\n    25\t| The epic distinguishes Codex/Copilot-backed GPT lanes from Claude-only fallback, but the harness terminology needs to preserve Claude Code as the always-present primary harness. | AC-2.2, AC-2.3, Role Model Matrix | Use `primary_harness: \"claude-code\"` plus per-role `secondary_harness: \"codex\" | \"copilot\" | \"none\"`. `none` means the role uses the primary harness only. | Resolved — clarified |\n    26\t| The epic says the CLI should be stateless across calls, but story implementation also requires retained implementor continuity. | Constraint C3, AC-4.2, AC-6.3 | Keep orchestration state outside the CLI. The CLI may emit continuation/session handles for provider sessions, but the orchestrator owns persistence, routing, and recovery through `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and any future orchestrator-managed support files. | Resolved — clarified |\n    27\t| The epic allows Copilot as the generic GPT fallback, but v1 Copilot support is limited to fresh-session roles while story implementation requires retained continuation. | AC-2.2, AC-4.2, Role Model Matrix | In v1, disallow Copilot for `story_implementor`. Retained implementor fallback order is Codex -> Claude Code primary harness. Copilot remains valid for fresh verifier, quick-fix, and synthesis roles. | Resolved — deviated |\n    28\t| The epic requires the packaged skill to include the CLI, but the current build surface only composes markdown skill outputs and bundled references. | Feature overview, CLI responsibilities implied by scope | Add a build step that bundles the modular CLI source into a single Node-compatible JS file and copies that file into the packaged skill output. | Resolved — deviated |\n    29\t\n    30\t### Tech Design Question Coverage\n    31\t\n    32\tThe epic’s Tech Design Questions are answered by this pack as follows:\n    33\t\n    34\t| Question | Primary Answer Location |\n    35\t|---|---|\n    36\t| Public CLI commands, arguments, exit codes, result formats | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Command Surface, Result Contracts |\n    37\t| Prompt assets storage and assembly | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Prompt Asset System |\n    38\t| Three self-review passes | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Story Implementation Flow |\n    39\t| Model matrix and harness fallback | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Run Config + Provider Adapters |\n    40\t| Continuation identifiers | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Session Continuation Contracts |\n    41\t| Exact result fields and serialization | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Result Contracts |\n    42\t| `team-impl-log.md` state model | `tech-design-skill-process.md` — Orchestrator State and Durable Artifacts |\n    43\t| How orchestrator consumes CLI results | `tech-design-skill-process.md` — Process Playbook and Stage Transitions |\n    44\t| Verification-gate hardening | `tech-design.md` — Verification Scripts; `tech-design-skill-process.md` — Initialization Flow |\n    45\t| Companion tech-design naming convention | This index — document structure and companion naming |\n    46\t| Implementor replacement vs continuation | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Story Continue Rules |\n    47\t| Quick-fix narrow contract | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Quick Fix Workflow |\n    48\t| Pre-epic cleanup artifacts | `tech-design-skill-process.md` and `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Epic Cleanup |\n    49\t| Self-review prompt evolution and stop rules | `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` — Story Implementation Flow |\n    50\t\n    51\t## Context\n    52\t\n    53\t`liminal-spec` is primarily a content-and-build repository, not a general application runtime. Most skills in the repo are markdown-first artifacts composed from `src/` and packaged into installable skill folders or standalone markdown files. That matters because `ls-claude-impl` is not a normal addition to the pack. It introduces a second kind of deliverable inside the same repo: a bundled runtime helper that must ship inside the packaged skill while still fitting the repository’s content-composition workflow.\n    54\t\n    55\tThe problem being solved is reliability of implementation orchestration, not lack of methodology. Existing implementation skill surfaces carry useful process ideas, but they rely on too much live runtime improvisation and too many layers between the orchestrator and the actual work. The new design narrows the system to three stable public surfaces. First, the skill teaches the orchestrator the process and the reading journey. Second, the orchestrator owns state and decision-making. Third, the CLI executes bounded operations and returns disciplined machine-readable results. The center of gravity shifts away from “agent teams all the way down” and toward “one orchestrator with explicit process docs and a legible execution surface.”\n    56\t\n    57\tThe future direction also matters now. The CLI is not just a local helper for this skill. It is an early public execution surface that is expected to map cleanly into a later SDK and then into web-app harness tools. That creates strong pressure toward explicit inputs, outputs, and outcome states. Hidden orchestration state inside the CLI would make later extraction harder. For the same reason, the skill should own the explanation of the process while the CLI owns execution. The orchestrator needs to understand what is happening at the process level without carrying implementation detail that belongs inside the runtime.\n    58\t\n    59\tA second pressure comes from prompt architecture. This feature does not only need CLI commands; it needs stable role prompts, reusable snippets, public insert hooks, and role-fit reading journeys. Those prompt assets are implementation-critical. They are not just docs, and they are not just helper strings. They are part of the runtime contract. The design therefore treats the prompt system as a first-class architectural surface rather than as glue hidden inside an implementation file.\n    60\t\n    61\tFinally, the repo’s existing build pipeline constrains how the feature lands. The current build emits markdown skill directories plus standalone markdown packs. It does not yet package executable runtime files into skill outputs. The design therefore includes build-system changes as part of the feature instead of leaving them to Story 0 discovery. The packaged skill must contain everything the orchestrator needs at runtime: markdown guidance plus a single bundled Node-compatible CLI file.\n    62\t\n    63\t## System View\n    64\t\n    65\tThere is no separate technical architecture document for this work. The top-tier surfaces below are therefore locally derived from the epic, nearby design notes, the existing `codex-impl` runtime, and the current repository layout.\n    66\t\n    67\t### Top-Tier Surfaces\n    68\t\n    69\t| Surface | Source | This Epic's Role |\n    70\t|---|---|---|\n    71\t| Skill Surface | Locally derived | `ls-claude-impl` markdown entrypoint and progressive-disclosure references that onboard the orchestrator and teach the process |\n    72\t| Runtime Surface | Locally derived | `ls-impl-cli` command surface, result contracts, provider adapters, prompt assembly, and packaging |\n    73\t| Build Surface | Locally derived | Manifest/build integration that composes the skill and embeds the bundled CLI artifact into packaged outputs |\n    74\t\n    75\t### System Context Diagram\n    76\t\n    77\t```mermaid\n    78\tflowchart LR\n    79\t    User[Human user] --> CC[Claude Code orchestrator]\n    80\t    CC --> Skill[ls-claude-impl SKILL.md]\n    81\t    Skill --> RefA[reading-journey.md]\n    82\t    Skill --> RefB[process-playbook.md]\n    83\t    Skill --> RefC[cli-operations.md]\n    84\t    Skill --> RefD[prompt-system.md]\n    85\t\n    86\t    CC --> SpecPack[Spec pack root]\n    87\t    SpecPack --> Epic[epic.md]\n    88\t    SpecPack --> TD[tech-design*.md]\n    89\t    SpecPack --> TP[test-plan.md]\n    90\t    SpecPack --> Stories[stories/]\n    91\t    SpecPack --> RunConfig[impl-run.config.json]\n    92\t    SpecPack --> Log[team-impl-log.md]\n    93\t\n    94\t    CC --> CLI[ls-impl-cli]\n    95\t    CLI --> PromptAssets[Embedded base prompts + snippets]\n    96\t    CLI --> Codex[Codex CLI]\n    97\t    CLI --> Copilot[Copilot CLI]\n    98\t    CLI --> Claude[Claude Code CLI]\n    99\t\n   100\t    CLI --> Results[Structured JSON results]\n   101\t    Results --> CC\n   102\t    CC --> Log\n   103\t```\n   104\t\n   105\t### External Contracts\n   106\t\n   107\tThe external contracts are narrower than the whole feature.
